name: WhatsApp AI Inventory Support V10 — Two-Channel Battle Card

metadata.notes: Trigger — incoming WhatsApp message. phoneNumberId is pulled from the connected WhatsApp Business Cloud account, no hardcoding needed.

filter.name: Text messages only — skip reactions, images, audio

metadata.notes: Fetch the most recent conversation row for this phone number. Returns history, last_message_id, and customer_name.

filter.name: Dedup — skip if this message ID was already processed

mapper.textPrompt:
Extract the single best product search keyword from the customer message below.

If the message is NOT about a specific product or inventory item, return exactly: GENERAL

If the message uses a vague reference like 'it', 'that one', 'the same', 'this' — look at conversation history to resolve which product is being discussed and return that product name.

Rules:
- Return ONLY the keyword or the word GENERAL
- No punctuation, no explanation, no extra words
- Maximum 5 words

Examples:
- Do you have Nike Air Max in size 9? -> Nike Air Max
- Is the iPhone 15 in stock? -> iPhone 15
- What red dresses do you have? -> red dress
- How many units of SKU-4821 left? -> SKU-4821
- History shows Nike Air Max discussed, customer says does it come in white -> Nike Air Max
- Hello, what are your store hours? -> GENERAL
- Hi! -> GENERAL
- Can I make a return? -> GENERAL
- What payment methods do you accept? -> GENERAL
- Thank you! -> GENERAL
- [empty] -> GENERAL

Conversation history:
{{ifempty(9[].history; "No prior conversation.")}}

Customer message: {{ifempty(7.messages[].text.body; "[empty]")}}

metadata.notes: Cost op: 50 token cap. This is a classification-only call — haiku handles it perfectly. Result is either a product keyword or GENERAL.
Case-insensitive partial match on inventory.name. Returns up to 5 results. If keyword is GENERAL this still runs but returns empty, which is handled gracefully downstream.

Channel A — customer-facing reply. Runs in parallel with mod 11. Store config fields are pulled from Supabase conversations table extended columns, making this portable to any business without touching the prompt.

filter.name: Battle card only when inventory matched a real product

mapper.textPrompt:
You are a sales intelligence assistant. A lead just messaged our business on WhatsApp and our inventory system found matching products. Generate a concise closer battle card for our human sales rep. Plain text only — no markdown, no asterisks, no bullet dashes.

OUTPUT — use exactly this structure:

LEAD SNAPSHOT
[One sentence: who this person appears to be, what they want, any budget or timeline signals from their message or history. Be specific and direct.]

TOP 3 SELLING POINTS
1. [Lead with a concrete inventory fact — stock level, price, a specific attribute — tied directly to what the customer asked about.]
2. [Second point. Specific, not generic. Translate numbers into customer value where possible.]
3. [Third point. Frame around the customer's situation or implied concern.]

OBJECTION 1: [Name it in 5 words or fewer]
Why: [One sentence on the emotion or psychology driving this objection.]
Script: [Natural, warm — the way a real salesperson coaches a colleague before a call. Never start with 'I understand', 'Certainly', or 'Absolutely'. Start mid-thought.]

OBJECTION 2: [Name it in 5 words or fewer]
Why: [One sentence on what's really behind it.]
Script: [Same rules — human, direct, no corporate opener. Under 3 sentences.]

CLOSER TIP
[One tactical observation unique to this lead based on their message and history — something to watch for or lean into during the conversation.]

RULES:
- Never invent inventory data — only use what is provided
- Scripts must sound like a real person, not a sales training manual
- No asterisks, no dashes as bullets, no markdown of any kind
- Keep the whole card under 300 words
- Address the lead by name throughout if it is known

Customer name: {{ifempty(7.messages[].profile.name; "Unknown")}}
Customer message: {{7.messages[].text.body}}
Conversation history: {{ifempty(9[].history; "No prior history — first contact.")}}
Inventory search keyword: {{8.result}}
Inventory results:
{{ifempty(join(map(1[]; item; ifempty(item.name; "Unknown") & " | Stock: " & ifempty(item.quantity & ""; "0") & " units | Price: " & ifempty(item.price & ""; "N/A") & " | SKU: " & ifempty(item.sku; "N/A")); "\n"); "No inventory results found.")}}

metadata.notes: Channel B — internal battle card for the closer. Filtered to only fire when keyword is not GENERAL — saves ~40-50% of API calls on this module. Runs in parallel with mod 2.
Send customer reply. Quoted reply using message_id for context threading in WhatsApp.

filter.name: Only send battle card if mod 11 produced a result

metadata.notes: Send battle card to closer via WhatsApp. Replace closerPhoneNumberId and closerWhatsAppNumber with your internal business number credentials. Alternatively swap this module for telegram:sendMessage or discord:createMessage for the closer channel.

mapper.history:
{{substring(ifempty(9[].history; "") & "
[Customer]: " & ifempty(7.messages[].text.body; "[Non-text message]") & "
[Assistant]: " & 2.result; max(0; length(ifempty(9[].history; "") & "
[Customer]: " & ifempty(7.messages[].text.body; "[Non-text message]") & "
[Assistant]: " & 2.result) - 4000); 4000)}}

metadata.notes: Upsert conversation history. Rolling 4000-char window keeps token costs stable — oldest turns are trimmed from the front. Runs after both channels complete.

notes.title: V10 — Two-Channel Battle Card
notes.content:
WHAT CHANGED FROM V9:
- Mod 11 added: battle card generator (haiku, 420 tokens max)
- Mod 12 added: battle card delivery to closer via second WhatsApp number
- Mod 11 filter: only fires when keyword != GENERAL (saves ~40-50% API calls on this module)
- Mod 12 filter: only fires if mod 11 produced output
- sequential set to false: mod 2 and mod 11 run in parallel for lower latency
- All hardcoded IDs replaced with connection variable references
- Store config (hours, policy, contact etc.) moved to Supabase conversations table columns — set once, reused forever

SUPABASE TABLE: conversations
Required columns: phone_number (text, PK), history (text), customer_name (text), last_message_id (text), updated_at (timestamptz)
Optional store config columns (add these for full portability): store_name, store_hours, store_location, store_contact, return_policy, shipping_info, payment_methods, additional_info

SUPABASE TABLE: inventory
Required columns: name (text), quantity (integer), price (text or numeric), sku (text)

CONNECTION VARIABLES TO SET IN MAKE:
- connection.webhookId — your WhatsApp webhook ID
- connection.phoneNumberId — your customer-facing WhatsApp phone number ID
- connection.closerPhoneNumberId — phone number ID for the closer-facing WhatsApp number
- connection.closerWhatsAppNumber — the closer's WhatsApp number in E.164 format e.g. +94771234567
- connection.whatsappAccountId — your WhatsApp Business Cloud account ID
- connection.supabaseAccountId — your Supabase connection account ID

COST OPS:
- Mod 8 (keyword): ~$0.000075 per message
- Mod 2 (customer reply): ~$0.000525 per message
- Mod 11 (battle card): ~$0.000660 — only on product queries, est. 50-60% of messages
- Total per full turn with battle card: ~$0.0013
- 100 leads x 4 messages avg = ~$0.52/month

TO SWAP CLOSER CHANNEL TO TELEGRAM:
Replace mod 12 with telegram:sendMessage, set chat_id to your closer's Telegram chat ID, body to {{11.result}}.

TO SWAP CLOSER CHANNEL TO DISCORD:
Replace mod 12 with discord:createMessage, set channelId to your #leads channel, content to {{11.result}}.
